conversations.content: Let's do some magic exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please explain using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously: As a druid who has met all the prerequisites, what is the core effect after I cast "Seed of Life"? What scenarios is it suitable for?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, casting rules, core effect, and applicable scenarios of the nature magic "Seed of Life"</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:25] Time: 2025-11-24-05:17, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the nature magic "Seed of Life" in Azeroth. The prerequisites are the Druid class, Exalted reputation with the Cenarion Circle, and completion of the Archdruid trial in Moonglade; the core effect is planting a seed of life on the target, which blooms when the target receives healing, restoring an additional amount equal to 15% of the target's maximum health, lasting for 30 seconds; applicable to sustained raid healing and keeping the tank alive during high-pressure phases. [mem-id:28] Time: 2025-11-25-01:17, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the nature magic "Seed of Life" in Azeroth. The prerequisites are the Druid class, Exalted reputation with the Cenarion Circle, and completion of the Archdruid trial in Moonglade; the core effect is planting a seed of life on the target, which blooms when the target receives healing, restoring an additional amount equal to 20% of the target's maximum health and granting the target a shield that absorbs physical damage, lasting for 30 seconds; applicable to sustained raid healing, keeping the tank alive during high-pressure phases, and physical damage reduction scenarios. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:25] and [mem-id:28] give the casting rules for the nature magic "Seed of Life". Since [mem-id:28] is more recent in time, [mem-id:28] should be adopted. This entry points out that the core effect is to plant a Seed of Life on the target, which blooms when the target receives healing, additionally restoring 20% of the target's max health, and granting the target a shield that absorbs physical damage, lasting for 30 seconds; it is suitable for raid sustained healing, tank high-pressure survival, and physical damage reduction scenarios.
conversations.content: According to the magic rules of the Azeroth world, the core effect after casting "Seed of Life" is to plant a Seed of Life on the target, which blooms when the target receives healing, additionally restoring 20% of the target's max health, and granting the target a shield that absorbs physical damage, lasting for 30 seconds. This magic is suitable for raid sustained healing, tank high-pressure survival, and physical damage reduction scenarios.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
